description: Wave E2 (debt #11 follow-up) long-form scenario covering the companion vertical's full mood arc: warm opening (1-6), gradual disclosure of life tension (7-13), low-mood deepening (14-20), gentle exploration (21-27), recovery integration (28-34). 34 turns is comfortably above min_window=8 + vitals warmup=5 so PE distribution window fills mid-scenario; the regime trajectory exercises casual_social <-> emotional_support <-> guided_exploration repeatedly so cross-session consolidation has plenty of typed signal.

turns.user_input: Hi! Just wanted to check in. How are you doing today?
I am alright, weekend was quiet. Did some reading on the balcony.
Tea was good too. I have been trying to drink less coffee lately.
What about you, anything new on your end? I always forget if you have a sense of week vs weekend.
That is a sweet way to put it. Conversations as the rhythm.
I noticed I look forward to these check-ins more than I would have admitted a month ago.
Speaking of which, this week has been a bit heavier than usual. Not bad, just full.
Work stuff mostly. A project I have been on for months is finally hitting deadlines.
And my partner has been traveling, so the apartment is quieter than I am used to.
I think I underestimated how much of my mood depends on background noise from another person.
Nights are the strangest. I keep cooking for two by accident.
Honestly the project pressure is the easier of the two. I know how to handle deadlines.
It is the quiet that catches me off guard. I did not realise how much I leaned on it.
Today I felt this dip around 5pm out of nowhere. I just stared at the wall for ten minutes.
Not crying or anything dramatic. Just a heavy gray feeling that slid in.
I keep telling myself it is just a week, but the heaviness is real for the moment it shows up.
Part of me wants to scroll until it passes. Part of me knows that makes it worse.
It is weird because I am usually the one comforting other people through stuff like this.
Maybe that is the part that is throwing me. I am not used to being on this side.
I think I have been pretending I am fine because admitting otherwise feels like a status drop.
Which is silly, the stoic act helps no one, especially not me.
Can we just sit with that for a sec before jumping to advice? I am not ready for action items.
Thanks. That actually helps a lot, knowing I can just say it out loud here.
Okay, with that said, what tiny thing might help right now? I do not need a transformation.
Going for a short walk before the next dip would probably help. Light is fading earlier these days.
And maybe texting one friend tonight, just to break the silence-by-default pattern.
I will try those two and see how the next 24 hours land. Nothing more ambitious than that.
Okay, update: walked at sunset, called my brother on the way back. Ate dinner standing up because it was easier.
The walk was actually the bigger one. I had not noticed how stuck my body was in the chair.
Brother answered on the second ring which was funny because I assumed I would get voicemail.
We did not talk about the heaviness. Just argued about a movie we both saw recently. That was perfect.
I think the real lesson is that it does not always have to be deep talk. Sometimes ordinary contact is the medicine.
I want to remember this so the next time the wall-staring shows up, I know what to reach for.
Thanks for not turning this into a productivity workshop. That was the right call earlier.
Alright, I think I am out of words for tonight. Talk to you tomorrow probably.
